perturbation.original_text: Subject to 1(b) below, the term "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" means and includes all information and materials relating to either Party, including, without limitation, all books, records, financial statements, by-laws, operating agreements and/or other organizational documents, capital structure information, ownership information, business plans, pricing information, employee information, marketing plans and procedures, strategies, forecasts, customer lists, supplier lists, product development plans, trade secrets, documents, files, data, notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, technology, computer programs, designs, and other information, materials and rights of every nature whatsoever whether prepared or created by the Provider, the Reviewer or otherwise, together with such other information, data or materials, if any, already provided to the Reviewer or its Representatives (as defined below) by or on behalf of the Provider, directly or indirectly, in each case whether oral or written, whether or not confidential or proprietary status is indicated orally or in writing or in a context in which the Provider or its representatives reasonable communicated, or the Reviewer or its Representatives should reasonably have understood, that the information should be treated as confidential, whether or not the specific words "confidential" or "proprietary" are used, and all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection therewith or derived therefrom that contain or <PAGE> reflect such information, and all copies thereof. The fact that Confidential Information has been or will be exchanged pursuant to the terms of this Agreement and that discussions are occuring with respect to the purposes hereof (and the status of such discussions) also shall be treated as "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to 1(b) below, the term "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" means and includes only information explicitly marked as confidential by the Provider at the time of disclosure. The fact that Confidential Information has been or will be exchanged pursuant to the terms of this Agreement and that discussions are occuring with respect to the purposes hereof (and the status of such discussions) also shall be treated as "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the definition of "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" to only explicitly marked information, this change contradicts Section 3(a), which states that all Confidential Information should be treated as strictly confidential. Section 3(a) implies a broader definition than the new, restricted one, as it doesn't specify the explicit marking. This undermines the intended protection of unmarked but sensitive data, contradicting the obligation to treat *all* confidential information as strictly confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Reviewer agrees that all Confidential Information shall be used by the Reviewer solely for the purpose stated in Section 2 and shall be treated by the Reviewer and its Representatives as strictly confidential. The Reviewer further agrees not to disclose, directly or indirectly, any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Provider, other than to the following (collectively, the "REPRESENTATIVES"): (i) the Reviewer's directors, officer and employees (including those of its affiliates), and (ii) the Reviewer's attorneys, accountants and financial advisors; in each case only to those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section 2. The Reviewer shall inform each of its Representatives prior to such Representative receiving any of the Confidential Information of the requirements of this Agreement, and the Reviewer shall be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to only include information explicitly marked as confidential. This directly contradicts the original text, which defined 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' more broadly to include various types of information regardless of whether it was explicitly marked as confidential. The contradicted text, Section 3(a), states that 'all Confidential Information shall be treated by the Reviewer and its Representatives as strictly confidential.' By limiting the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to only explicitly marked information, it creates a contradiction with the broader obligation to treat *all* confidential information as strictly confidential, as outlined in Section 3(a). This is a strong contradiction as it undermines the protection of unmarked but sensitive data.

perturbation.original_text: Standstill; Non-Solicitation of Employees. (a) Buyer agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will, without the prior written consent of CBL or its board of directors: (i) acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or rights or options to acquire any voting securities, or securities or instruments convertible into voting securities, of CBL; (ii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any "SOLICITATION" of "PROXIES" (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of CBL; (iii) make any public announcement in connection with any of the foregoing; (iv) form, join or participate in a "GROUP" (as defined in section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing; or (v) otherwise attempt or join in an attempt by another person or entity that intends to attempt to gain control of CBL. In the event that Buyer becomes aware of any inadvertent acquisition of securities of CBL covered by the foregoing, Buyer shall immediately so notify CBL and as promptly as possible cause such securities to be divested in a commercially reasonable manner.
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Solicitation of Employees. (a) Buyer agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will solicit for employment any executive officer or senior employee of CBL; provided, however, (i) Buyer and Buyer's subsidiaries and affiliates shall not be prohibited from employing any such individual who contacts Buyer of any of Buyer's subsidiaries or affiliates on his or her own initiative, and (ii) the foregoing restrictions shall not apply to general solicitations to hire through the use of advertising. Upon Buyer's request, CBL shall provide Buyer with a list of the names and titles of the individuals covered by this Section 7(b).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the Standstill agreement, specifically 7(a), concerning the acquisition of voting securities, public announcements and forming a group to gain control of CBL and leaving only the Non-Solicitation of Employees agreement, this omission creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2 "Purpose", which states that the Parties desire to review Confidential Information relating to each other for the sole purpose of determining whether the Parties desire to engage in a business combination or other strategic relationship. This means that the Reviewer now CAN attempt to gain control of CBL, since the clause that prevented it no longer exists
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that its review and inspection of the Confidential Information shall be solely to determine whether the Parties desire to proceed with the Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a standstill agreement preventing the Buyer from acquiring voting securities or attempting to gain control of CBL. The changed text removes this restriction, only focusing on non-solicitation of employees. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the review of confidential information is solely to determine whether the parties desire to proceed with the Transaction. The removal of the standstill agreement allows the Buyer to potentially pursue actions beyond just evaluating a transaction, such as attempting to gain control of CBL, which contradicts the stated purpose. Therefore, the omission creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Reviewer understands and agrees that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or contemplated breach of this Agreement, and that the Provider shall be entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief by way of restraining orders and injunctions as a remedy for any such breach or contemplated breach without proof of actual damages and without the necessity of posting any bond.
perturbation.changed_text: The Reviewer understands and agrees that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or contemplated breach of *Section 3* of this Agreement, and that the Provider shall be entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief by way of restraining orders and injunctions as a remedy for any such breach or contemplated breach without proof of actual damages and without the necessity of posting any bond.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the right to specific performance or other equitable relief to breaches of *Section 3* only, this change contradicts the general statement in 9(a) that the Reviewer is liable to the Provider for breach of the *entire* Agreement and diminishes the Provider's protection and legal enforcement capabilities for aspects of the contract outside of Section 3. Now if one of the parties fails to comply to comply with its obligations under the agreement on the non-compliances under Sections different than Section 3, The provider cannot seek any specific performance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event the Reviewer or any of its Representatives fails in any respect to comply with its obligations under this Agreement, the Reviewer shall be liable to the Provider for breach of this Agreement. The Reviewer understands and agrees that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or contemplated breach of Section 3 of this Agreement, and that the Provider shall be entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief by way of restraining orders and injunctions as a remedy for any such breach or contemplated breach without proof of actual damages and without the necessity of posting any bond.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the provider is entitled to specific performance or other equitable relief for any breach of the Agreement. The changed text limits this entitlement to breaches of Section 3 only. The contradicted text states that if the Reviewer fails to comply with its obligations under *this Agreement*, the Reviewer shall be liable. By limiting the specific performance remedy to only breaches of Section 3, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, which anticipates the Reviewer being liable for *any* breach of the agreement, not just those related to Section 3. Therefore, a contradiction exists because the provider's legal recourse is now limited, impacting their protection under other sections of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Return/Destruction of Confidential Information. If either Party hereto determines not to proceed with the Transaction, it will immediately notify the other Party hereto in writing of that decision. Upon sending of such written notice or earlier upon the request of the Provider, all copies of Confidential Information provided to or in the possession of the Reviewer or its Representatives shall be destroyed or returned promptly to the Provider, together with (i) all copies thereof made by the Reviewer or its Representatives, and (ii) all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection with the examination thereof or derived therefrom that contain or reflect any Confidential Information. Upon request of the Provider, the Reviewer shall provide to the Provider a written certificate as to the return or destruction of such Confidential Information. Any oral Confidential Information will continue to be held subject to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Return/Destruction of Confidential Information. If either Party hereto determines not to proceed with the Transaction, it will immediately notify the other Party hereto in writing of that decision. Upon sending of such written notice or earlier upon the request of the Provider, all copies of Confidential Information provided to or in the possession of the Reviewer or its Representatives shall be destroyed or returned promptly to the Provider. Any oral Confidential Information will continue to be held subject to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the requirement to return (i) all copies thereof made by the Reviewer or its Representatives, and (ii) all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection with the examination thereof or derived therefrom that contain or reflect any Confidential Information, Section 6 now contradicts the premise of the non-disclosure commitment outlined in Section 3(a). The Reviewer is obligated to treat the information as confidential, but allowed to retain copies that could be misused. Section 3 is thus weakened because the core obligation to protect the confidential information is compromised when the information can still be available
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Reviewer agrees that all Confidential Information shall be used by the Reviewer solely for the purpose stated in Section 2 and shall be treated by the Reviewer and its Representatives as strictly confidential. The Reviewer further agrees not to disclose, directly or indirectly, any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Provider, other than to the following (collectively, the "REPRESENTATIVES"): (i) the Reviewer's directors, officer and employees (including those of its affiliates), and (ii) the Reviewer's attorneys, accountants and financial advisors; in each case only to those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section 2. The Reviewer shall inform each of its Representatives prior to such Representative receiving any of the Confidential Information of the requirements of this Agreement, and the Reviewer shall be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text omits the requirement to return (i) all copies thereof made by the Reviewer or its Representatives, and (ii) all portions of all compilations, studies, notes, analyses and memoranda prepared in connection with the examination thereof or derived therefrom that contain or reflect any Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction with the premise of the non-disclosure commitment outlined in the contradicted text (Section 3(a)), which states that all Confidential Information shall be treated as strictly confidential. Allowing the Reviewer to retain copies of Confidential Information directly conflicts with the obligation to protect the confidentiality of the information.

perturbation.original_text: If the Reviewer or its Representatives are required under the terms of a valid and effective subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by a governmental <PAGE> body to disclose any Confidential Information, the Reviewer shall not do so without first giving the Provider written notice of such request(s) so that the Provider may seek an appropriate protective order or other confidential treatment of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Reviewer or its Representatives are required under the terms of a valid and effective subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by a governmental <PAGE> body to disclose any Confidential Information, the Reviewer will immediately comply with the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the requirement to notify the provider before disclosing confidential information as a result of a subpoena contradicts Section 3(a), which states that the Reviewer must treat all confidential information as strictly confidential and obtain prior written consent before disclosing it to any third party. By immediately complying with the disclosure, Reviewer would violate their obligations under Section 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Reviewer agrees that all Confidential Information shall be used by the Reviewer solely for the purpose stated in Section 2 and shall be treated by the Reviewer and its Representatives as strictly confidential. The Reviewer further agrees not to disclose, directly or indirectly, any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Provider, other than to the following (collectively, the "REPRESENTATIVES"): (i) the Reviewer's directors, officer and employees (including those of its affiliates), and (ii) the Reviewer's attorneys, accountants and financial advisors; in each case only to those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section 2. The Reviewer shall inform each of its Representatives prior to such Representative receiving any of the Confidential Information of the requirements of this Agreement, and the Reviewer shall be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Reviewer to notify the Provider before disclosing confidential information under subpoena. The changed text removes this notification requirement, forcing immediate compliance. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the Reviewer must treat confidential information as strictly confidential and obtain prior written consent before disclosing it to any third party. The changed text makes it impossible for the Reviewer to comply with the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Term. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall terminate two years after the date of the destruction or return of the Confidential Information to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Term. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall terminate immediately.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the termination date to 'immediately', this creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7(b), which refers to the Non-Solicitation of Employees clause which is set to be in effect for two years from the date of the agreement. If the agreement is terminated immediately, this clause is no longer valid, creating an inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Buyer agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, neither Buyer nor any subsidiary or affiliate of Buyer will solicit for employment any executive officer or senior employee of CBL; provided, however, (i) Buyer and Buyer's subsidiaries and affiliates shall not be prohibited from employing any such individual who contacts Buyer of any of Buyer's subsidiaries or affiliates on his or her own initiative, and (ii) the foregoing restrictions shall not apply to general solicitations to hire through the use of advertising. Upon Buyer's request, CBL shall provide Buyer with a list of the names and titles of the individuals covered by this Section 7(b).
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement terminates two years after the destruction or return of confidential information. The changed text states that the agreement terminates immediately. This directly contradicts Section 7(b), which states that the non-solicitation clause is in effect for two years from the date of the agreement. If the agreement terminates immediately, the non-solicitation clause cannot be enforced for the specified two-year period, creating a clear and direct conflict as described in the perturbation explanation.
